{{Infobox character
|nom=Mouse
|apparition=Appears in book 1
|status=Unknown
}}

== Biography ==

The Mouse is a creature encountered by Alice in a pool in Wonderland. When Alice addressed it with inquiries, the Mouse responded with apparent interest and seemed to wink with one of its eyes, though Alice could not determine whether it understood English. Given its possible incomprehension, Alice speculated that the Mouse was French and had come with William the Conqueror.

The Mouse subsequently established itself as a figure of authority among the wet animals gathered to dry themselves. It called them to sit and listen, speaking with an important air and promising to make them dry enough. This remedy it undertook through what it considered the driest approach available—a recitation of the history of William the Conqueror.

== Relationships ==

'''[[Alice]]''' — wary alliance. When Alice offers to avoid discussing cats and dogs, the Mouse responds by swimming slowly back, suggesting an uneasy, tentative understanding rather than genuine rapport.

'''[[Dinah]]''' — enemy. Based on the Mouse's statement that its family has "always hated cats: nasty, low, vulgar things," the Mouse seemingly harbors animosity toward Dinah rooted in her feline nature.

''Evolution:''
* [[Alice]] — Wary alliance (ch.2→ch.3)
* [[Dinah]] — Enemy (ch.2)
== References ==

- Alice's Adventures in Wonderland

[[Category:Characters]]
[[Category:Secondary Characters]]
[[Category:Personnages du Tome 1]]
